session: SuperBEST v4 — Biology & Epidemiology Cost Session

equations.name: Logistic Growth
equations.domain: Population biology, ecology, epidemiology (early epidemic growth)
cross_domain: Logistic function (ML sigmoid): σ(x) = 1/(1+exp(-x)) — 4n (DEML + add_pos + recip)
Verhulst equation in ecology shares identical structure
Any 'saturation' S-curve: 10n canonical form
equations.key_observation: Logistic growth is 10n — the S-curve of nature, from tumor growth to epidemic saturation.

equations.name: SIR Epidemic Model — Per-Timestep Update
equations.formula: dS/dt = -β·S·I/N;  dI/dt = β·S·I/N - γ·I;  dR/dt = γ·I

breakdown.note: recovery term; shared for dI and dR

equations.sharing_note: With sharing of βSI/N and γI across all three equations; standalone would be ~24n
cross_domain: SEIR adds E compartment (~+6n per step)
SIS (no recovery to R): 12n per step
equations.key_observation: SIR is 20n per step — cheap enough to run millions of timesteps; epidemic simulation is computationally tractable.

equations.name: Basic Reproduction Number
equations.domain: Epidemiology — threshold for epidemic spread (R₀ > 1: epidemic; R₀ < 1: extinction)
cross_domain: Any ratio of rates: 2n
Signal-to-noise ratio, odds ratio in biostatistics — same 2n
The most important number in epidemiology costs only a single division
equations.key_observation: R₀ = 2n — the most important number in epidemiology is a single division. Elegant simplicity.

equations.name: Lotka-Volterra Predator-Prey
equations.formula: dN/dt = rN - αNP  (prey);  dP/dt = βNP - mP  (predator)
equations.sharing_note: N and P values shared; βN and αN partially overlap if β=α, else independent
cross_domain: Competitive exclusion (two-species): same bilinear structure
Chemical kinetics (bimolecular reaction): A+B→C has same dA/dt form
Bilinear interaction (NP term) is the signature: each equation 8n
equations.key_observation: Lotka-Volterra is 16n per step — two coupled 8n equations. Bilinear interaction (NP) is the structural signature.

equations.name: Malthusian (Exponential) Growth
equations.domain: Population biology — unconstrained growth; also epidemic exponential phase
cross_domain: Compound interest A = P·e^(rt): identical 5n
Beer-Lambert transmission I = I₀·e^(-μx): 5n (DEML variant: mul+DEML+mul = 2+1+2)
Radioactive decay N = N₀·e^(-λt): 5n (same, DEML variant)
Universal constant-rate exponential process: 5n
equations.key_observation: Malthusian growth = 5n — the universal cost of any constant-rate exponential process across all domains.

equations.name: Gompertz Growth
equations.domain: Tumor growth, actuarial mortality, biological aging, bacterial growth curves

breakdown.note: inner exponential via DEML; result is positive

equations.deml_chain_note: Two chained DEML calls elegantly handle the double-exponential: DEML∘DEML(b(t-tm)) = exp(-exp(-b(t-tm)))
cross_domain: Gumbel CDF (extreme value distribution): F(x) = exp(-exp(-(x-μ)/β)) — same 8n structure
Weibull-like growth curves: structurally similar
Actuarial Makeham-Gompertz mortality law
equations.key_observation: Gompertz is 8n — two chained DEML calls give the double-exponential cheaply. Also equals Gumbel CDF structure.

equations.name: Hill Equation (Dose-Response)
equations.domain: Pharmacology, biochemistry — receptor binding, enzyme kinetics (generalizes Michaelis-Menten)

n=1 (Michaelis-Menten).breakdown: add_pos(Km, S)=3n + div(S, sum)=2n + mul(Vmax, frac)=2n = 7n

cross_domain: Michaelis-Menten enzyme kinetics (n=1): 7n
Pythagorean expectation (baseball): W% = RS^k/(RS^k+RA^k) — 11n (two pows + add_pos + div)
Langmuir adsorption isotherm (n=1): 7n
Any sigmoidal binding/saturation with cooperativity: 13n
equations.key_observation: Hill equation is 13n; Michaelis-Menten (n=1) is only 7n — cooperativity (n>1) costs +6n via two pow operations.

equations.name: Infection Growth (Exponential Phase)
equations.formula: I(t) = I₀ · e^(λt)  where λ = β - γ
equations.domain: Early epidemic spread — before herd immunity effects; also used in R₀ estimation

breakdown.note: net growth rate λ (intrinsic epidemic growth)

cross_domain: Arrhenius rate with computed activation: k = A·exp(-(Ea/R)·(1/T)) — 7n with sub for rate
Any process where effective rate = difference of two rates: 7n
If λ is precomputed constant: reduces to Malthusian 5n
equations.key_observation: Infection growth is 7n — Malthusian 5n plus a 2n sub to compute the net rate. Same as Arrhenius with computed argument.

equations.name: Herd Immunity Threshold
equations.domain: Epidemiology — minimum vaccination fraction to prevent epidemic spread
cross_domain: Carnot efficiency η = 1 - T_cold/T_hot: 4n (div instead of recip, since T_cold ≠ 1)
Any '1 minus a fraction' threshold: 3n with recip, 4n with div
Vaccine efficacy complement: same 3n structure
equations.key_observation: Herd immunity threshold = 3n — a recip plus a sub. The policy threshold for vaccination campaigns is nearly free to compute.

equations.name: Allometric Scaling (Kleiber's Law)
equations.formula: Y = a · M^b  (b ≈ 3/4 for metabolic rate)
equations.domain: Biological scaling laws — metabolic rate, lifespan, heart rate vs body mass

breakdown.note: body mass to scaling exponent; b=3/4 for Kleiber

cross_domain: Stefan-Boltzmann law P = εσA·T^4: 5n (pow + mul)
Kepler's third law T² ∝ a³: 5n
Any power law Y = a·x^b: 5n — universal cost of scaling relationships
Fractal dimension measurement, surface-area-to-volume ratio
equations.key_observation: Allometric scaling = 5n — identical to Malthusian growth cost, but via pow not exp. Universal cost of any power-law relationship.

summary_table.name: Basic Reproduction Number R₀
summary_table.highlight: cheapest important metric in all of epidemiology

summary_table.name: Allometric Scaling (Kleiber)
summary_table.highlight: same 5n as Malthusian but via pow; all power laws

summary_table.name: Infection Growth (Exponential)
summary_table.highlight: Malthusian + sub for net rate computation

summary_table.name: Gompertz Growth
summary_table.highlight: double-exponential via two chained DEML calls

summary_table.name: Michaelis-Menten (Hill n=1)
summary_table.highlight: enzyme kinetics special case of Hill

summary_table.name: Lotka-Volterra (per step)
summary_table.highlight: two 8n coupled equations; bilinear NP term

summary_table.name: SIR Model (per timestep)
summary_table.highlight: 20n with sharing; epidemic simulation is tractable

key_insight: The most important number in epidemiology (R₀=2n) and the policy threshold derived from it (p_c=3n) are the cheapest equations in the entire biology/epidemiology set. Complex dynamics (SIR=20n, Lotka-Volterra=16n) cost an order of magnitude more but remain tractable for simulation.
